commits.subject: auto-add --access public for scoped packages on npm publish
commits.body:
Scoped packages (@org/name) default to private on npm and require
--access public on first publish. Bumpcraft now detects the @ prefix
and adds the flag automatically.
commits.raw:
0e13cba453362149b0a4289eb50de2a44fc5bf0d fix: auto-add --access public for scoped packages on npm publish
Scoped packages (@org/name) default to private on npm and require
--access public on first publish. Bumpcraft now detects the @ prefix
and adds the flag automatically.

## [1.5.7](https://github.com/ayush-jadaun/bumpcraft/releases/tag/v1.5.7) (2026-03-24) ([compare](https://github.com/ayush-jadaun/bumpcraft/compare/v1.5.6...v1.5.7))

commits.subject: comprehensive edge case fixes + 26 new tests (250 total)
commits.body:
Bugs fixed:
- Default plugins now include conventional-commits + changelog-md
  (empty plugins array = silent failure was the root cause of
  monorepo release not detecting commits)
- Empty commit subjects (feat:   ) rejected by parser
- workspace:* refs preserved in inter-package dep updater
- Name collision in workspace auto-detect: packages/cache/memory
  and packages/db/memory no longer overwrite each other
- pnpm-workspace.yaml with comments and unquoted globs parsed correctly
- package.json workspaces takes precedence over pnpm-workspace.yaml

26 new tests covering:
- Commit parser: no colon, empty scope, nested parens, feat!:,
  feat(scope)!:, BREAKING CHANGE footer, hyphenated scope, slashed
  scope, emoji, empty message, whitespace-only subject, merge commits,
  revert commits, multi-line, 500-char subject, markdown special chars,
  custom commitTypes, feat disabled via commitTypes
- Workspace detection: name collisions, comments in yaml, unquoted
  globs, package.json vs pnpm-workspace.yaml precedence, zero matches,
  missing package.json in dir, explicit config override, empty config

Docs updated: monorepo.md (pnpm support, workspace:* note, collisions),
llms.txt (default plugins, pnpm-workspace.yaml, workspace:* skip)

commits.subject: default plugins to conventional-commits + changelog-md when not specified
commits.body:
Bug: when .bumpcraftrc.json omitted the plugins field, it defaulted
to an empty array. No plugins = no commit parser = parsedCommits
stays empty = bumpType always 'none'. Users who only configured
commitTypes/policies without listing plugins got silent failures.

Fix: plugins default to ['bumpcraft-plugin-conventional-commits',
'bumpcraft-plugin-changelog-md'] — the two essential plugins that
make bumpcraft work out of the box.

Also removed debug logging from monorepo release.
commits.raw:
e86984585b38347d8fd3588e58c120115887ad43 fix: default plugins to conventional-commits + changelog-md when not specified
Bug: when .bumpcraftrc.json omitted the plugins field, it defaulted
to an empty array. No plugins = no commit parser = parsedCommits
stays empty = bumpType always 'none'. Users who only configured
commitTypes/policies without listing plugins got silent failures.

Fix: plugins default to ['bumpcraft-plugin-conventional-commits',
'bumpcraft-plugin-changelog-md'] — the two essential plugins that
make bumpcraft work out of the box.

Also removed debug logging from monorepo release.

commits.subject: validate and changelog commands now monorepo-aware
commits.body:
Bug: bumpcraft validate called runRelease() (single-package) even in
monorepo mode, which reads root package.json instead of per-package
versions. Unscoped feat: commits were processed but the root version
was already up-to-date, so it reported 'no release needed'.

Fix: validate and changelog commands now check config.monorepo and
call runMonorepoRelease() when in monorepo mode, matching the
behavior of the release command.
commits.raw:
b5a93e61ffb2636c7cb201443d8597479809186a fix: validate and changelog commands now monorepo-aware
Bug: bumpcraft validate called runRelease() (single-package) even in
monorepo mode, which reads root package.json instead of per-package
versions. Unscoped feat: commits were processed but the root version
was already up-to-date, so it reported 'no release needed'.

Fix: validate and changelog commands now check config.monorepo and
call runMonorepoRelease() when in monorepo mode, matching the
behavior of the release command.

commits.subject: changeset files and version PR mode (224 tests)
commits.body:
Changeset files (.changeset/*.md):
- bumpcraft changeset create: interactive or --package/--bump/--message
- bumpcraft changeset list: show pending changesets
- Changeset bumps merge with commit-based detection (highest wins)
- Changeset summaries appear in changelog under Changesets section
- Files are consumed (deleted) after successful non-dry-run release
- Supports multiple packages per changeset file

Version PR mode (--create-pr):
- Creates a bumpcraft/release-v{version} branch
- Commits release artifacts (package.json, CHANGELOG.md, .bumpcraft/)
- Opens a GitHub PR with changelog as the body
- Switches back to original branch after PR creation
- Alternative to --push for teams that want review before releasing

19 new tests:
- Changeset unit tests (10): create, read, parse, consume, edge cases
- Changeset integration tests (9): override/merge with commits,
  dry-run preservation, multiple files merge, no-downgrade,
  trigger release from chore-only commits

Docs added: changesets.md, version-pr.md
Docs updated: README.md, llms.txt, cli-reference.md

commits.subject: publish, hooks, workspace detect, linked packages, GitLab/Bitbucket plugins, custom templates, status command (205 tests)
commits.body:
New features:
- bumpcraft publish: npm publish with --provenance, --package, --tag, --otp, skips private
- bumpcraft status: show pending changes per package with predicted bump types
- Workspace auto-detect from npm/pnpm/yarn workspaces field (zero config monorepo)
- Lifecycle hooks: beforeRelease, afterRelease, beforeBump, afterBump with env vars
- Inter-package dependency tracking: auto-updates dep versions in dependent packages
- Linked packages: packages in a group always release at the same (highest) version
- GitLab release plugin (bumpcraft-plugin-gitlab)
- Bitbucket release plugin (bumpcraft-plugin-bitbucket)
- Custom changelog templates with {version}, {date}, {commits}, {previousVersion}
- Private package support: versioned but skipped by publish

Docs added: monorepo.md, hooks.md, publishing.md
Docs updated: cli-reference.md (status, publish, init-release), README.md, llms.txt

42 new tests covering:
- Hooks: unit (5) + integration (2) — runs, env vars, skips dry-run, abort on failure
- Plugins: GitLab (3) + Bitbucket (4) — skip conditions for missing creds/config
- Changelog template: custom placeholders, breaking flag, default fallback (3)
- Workspace auto-detect: npm array, yarn object, direct paths, empty, nonexistent (5)
- Inter-package deps: transitive chains, multiple dependents, devDeps+peerDeps (3)
- Linked packages: highest version wins, major+minor sync, independent unlinked (3)
- Private packages: still versioned during release (1)
- Monorepo edge cases: substring scope collision, nested scope, 30 commits to one pkg,
  interleaved commits, missing package.json, no version field, changelog isolation,
  commit to unknown scope, version divergence (13)

commits.subject: monorepo support with 29 comprehensive tests (163 total)
commits.body:
Monorepo release support:
- Config: monorepo.{name}.path + optional tagFormat
- Scoped commits only bump that package, unscoped apply to all
- Each package reads its own package.json version independently
- Per-package CHANGELOG.md, git tags, and history entries
- --package flag, --force-bump, --pre-release per-package
- CLI auto-detects monorepo from config
- Custom tag formats (auth@1.2.0 or @mono/auth@1.2.0)
- Breaking changes: scoped=one package, unscoped=all packages
commits.raw:
82b28c7807cd2e06e6884b2242a5cb97c2a35cd0 feat: monorepo support with 29 comprehensive tests (163 total)
Monorepo release support:
- Config: monorepo.{name}.path + optional tagFormat

- --package flag, --force-bump, --pre-release per-package
- CLI auto-detects monorepo from config
- Custom tag formats (auth@1.2.0 or @mono/auth@1.2.0)
- Breaking changes: scoped=one package, unscoped=all packages

commits.subject: --push adds [skip ci] to release commits
commits.body:
Release commits (chore(release): x.x.x [skip ci]) now skip all CI
workflows entirely. No more wasted test/lint/build runs on bot commits.

Removed the !contains('chore(release)') guard from CI since [skip ci]
handles it at the GitHub level — zero workflows triggered.

commits.subject: --push silently skips pushTag when tag already exists on remote
commits.body:
The GitHub plugin creates a release via API which auto-creates the
tag on remote. The --push flow then tried to push the same tag,
causing a rejected error. Now caught gracefully.
commits.raw:
c016b193cc941b1ff7b885fca248277f0ac8a039 fix: --push silently skips pushTag when tag already exists on remote
The GitHub plugin creates a release via API which auto-creates the
tag on remote. The --push flow then tried to push the same tag,
causing a rejected error. Now caught gracefully.

commits.subject: --push auto-creates GitHub release when GITHUB_TOKEN is set
commits.body:
No plugin config needed — when --push is used and GITHUB_TOKEN +
GITHUB_REPOSITORY env vars are available (auto-provided in GitHub
Actions), bumpcraft creates a GitHub Release with the full changelog
as the body. Zero secrets to configure for GitHub Actions users.

Docs updated: cli-reference.md, deployment.md, llms.txt

commits.subject: performance and robustness hardening
commits.body:
Performance:
- Cap first-release commit scan to 500 (prevents OOM on large repos
  with no tags). Repos with tags are unaffected.

Correctness:
- Commit hash regex now accepts uppercase hex [A-F] (some git configs
  and tools output uppercase hashes)
- Shallow clone detection: warns when fetch-depth is 1 in CI
- Dirty working tree warning on non-dry-run releases
- Tests for uppercase and mixed-case hex hashes (134 total)
commits.raw:
57f1a28cf45ab1fc55d222561b393e3e8f1d739d fix: performance and robustness hardening
Performance:
- Cap first-release commit scan to 500 (prevents OOM on large repos
  with no tags). Repos with tags are unaffected.

Correctness:
- Commit hash regex now accepts uppercase hex [A-F] (some git configs
  and tools output uppercase hashes)
- Shallow clone detection: warns when fetch-depth is 1 in CI
- Dirty working tree warning on non-dry-run releases
- Tests for uppercase and mixed-case hex hashes (134 total)

commits.subject: add edge case tests for all untested scenarios (132 total)
commits.body:
SemVer:
- Large version numbers (99.99.99, 999.0.0)
- Build metadata parsing, serialization, and stripping on bump
- Pre-release < release comparison
- Numeric < alphanumeric pre-release (spec §11.4.1)
- Build metadata ignored in comparison
- Fewer pre-release fields < more fields
- Tag switching resets counter
- Empty string and whitespace handling

Commit parsing:
- Squash merge with PR number: feat: thing (#42)
- Emoji in commit subject
- Extra whitespace trimming
- Garbage/partial input gracefully skipped

Integration:
- GitHub plugin skips silently without GITHUB_TOKEN
- Squash merge commits parsed end-to-end
- Build metadata round-trip
- 50 commits performance test
- init-release idempotency
commits.raw:
5e3a792762add2788f598971d0685269c607f03e test: add edge case tests for all untested scenarios (132 total)
SemVer:
- Large version numbers (99.99.99, 999.0.0)
- Build metadata parsing, serialization, and stripping on bump
- Pre-release < release comparison
- Numeric < alphanumeric pre-release (spec §11.4.1)
- Build metadata ignored in comparison
- Fewer pre-release fields < more fields
- Tag switching resets counter
- Empty string and whitespace handling

Commit parsing:
- Squash merge with PR number: feat: thing (#42)
- Emoji in commit subject
- Extra whitespace trimming
- Garbage/partial input gracefully skipped

Integration:
- GitHub plugin skips silently without GITHUB_TOKEN
- Squash merge commits parsed end-to-end
- Build metadata round-trip
- 50 commits performance test
- init-release idempotency

commits.subject: comprehensive edge case tests for all failure scenarios

  empty, merge commits, scoped with slashes, Co-Authored-By trailers,

commits.subject: init-release now commits version change before tagging
commits.body:
The tag must point to a commit that includes the updated package.json.
Previous version wrote package.json then tagged HEAD (which still had
the old version). Now: write → commit → tag → verify.

Previous version wrote package.json then tagged HEAD (which still had
the old version). Now: write → commit → tag → verify.

commits.subject: add init-release command for baseline tagging
commits.body:
Allows existing projects to adopt bumpcraft from a clean slate:
  bumpcraft init-release --version 1.0.0

Creates a git tag, updates package.json, records in release history.
Future releases only look at commits after the baseline tag.

Options: --force (overwrite tag), --allow-dirty, --message, --no-commit
commits.raw:
c1fba983ed3501c1feed4d351c1dcd2ff4ceb389 feat: add init-release command for baseline tagging
Allows existing projects to adopt bumpcraft from a clean slate:
  bumpcraft init-release --version 1.0.0

Creates a git tag, updates package.json, records in release history.
Future releases only look at commits after the baseline tag.

Options: --force (overwrite tag), --allow-dirty, --message, --no-commit

commits.subject: enable CI/CD pipeline — tests on push/PR, auto-release on main
commits.body:
- Tests run on Node 20 and 22 for every push and PR
- On main: bumpcraft releases itself (version bump, changelog, GitHub release)
- Commits release artifacts (package.json, CHANGELOG.md, .bumpcraft/)
- Publishes to npm automatically via NPM_TOKEN secret
- Skips release if no conventional commits since last tag

commits.subject: address seventh-round audit — dashboard build + query param safety
commits.body:
- I1: build script now copies src/dashboard/index.html to dist/ after
  tsc, so the dashboard actually exists in production/Docker deployments
  instead of silently returning 404
- I2: changelog and history API routes use queryString() helper to safely
  extract string-only query params; duplicate/array params are ignored
  instead of being unsafely cast to string
commits.raw:
f1c43f189087bd4155a2a9094303fdb4b88e001b fix: address seventh-round audit — dashboard build + query param safety
- I1: build script now copies src/dashboard/index.html to dist/ after
  tsc, so the dashboard actually exists in production/Docker deployments
  instead of silently returning 404
- I2: changelog and history API routes use queryString() helper to safely
  extract string-only query params; duplicate/array params are ignored
  instead of being unsafely cast to string

commits.subject: address sixth-round audit — last two stragglers
commits.body:
- I1: validate command's inline action handler now wrapped in try/catch
  matching all other CLI commands (was the only one missed in round 5)
- I2: docker-compose.yml passes GITHUB_TOKEN and GITHUB_REPOSITORY into
  the container environment so the GitHub release plugin actually fires
commits.raw:
e8e6cf93e47bca662f088984deb75539f523e2a9 fix: address sixth-round audit — last two stragglers
- I1: validate command's inline action handler now wrapped in try/catch
  matching all other CLI commands (was the only one missed in round 5)
- I2: docker-compose.yml passes GITHUB_TOKEN and GITHUB_REPOSITORY into
  the container environment so the GitHub release plugin actually fires

commits.subject: address fifth-round audit — final hardening
commits.body:
- I1: github plugin validates repo format against owner/name pattern
  to prevent SSRF via path traversal in the GitHub API URL
- I2: all 7 CLI command action handlers wrapped in try/catch with
  clean error messages and process.exit(1) instead of stack traces
- I3: --force-bump validates major/minor/patch in CLI; runRelease
  normalizes forceBump early so invalid values like 'none' are
  treated as undefined instead of crashing with internal error
- M1: Dockerfile adds non-root user (bumpcraft) via USER directive
- M2: Dockerfile uses --omit=dev instead of deprecated --only=production
- M3: Dockerfile adds HEALTHCHECK for orchestrator liveness detection
commits.raw:
8a39921cfdd1e5c6b23e1757b48313472609ff58 fix: address fifth-round audit — final hardening
- I1: github plugin validates repo format against owner/name pattern
  to prevent SSRF via path traversal in the GitHub API URL
- I2: all 7 CLI command action handlers wrapped in try/catch with
  clean error messages and process.exit(1) instead of stack traces
- I3: --force-bump validates major/minor/patch in CLI; runRelease
  normalizes forceBump early so invalid values like 'none' are
  treated as undefined instead of crashing with internal error
- M1: Dockerfile adds non-root user (bumpcraft) via USER directive
- M2: Dockerfile uses --omit=dev instead of deprecated --only=production
- M3: Dockerfile adds HEALTHCHECK for orchestrator liveness detection

commits.subject: address fourth-round audit — all remaining issues resolved
commits.body:
CRITICAL:
- C1: HistoryStore.writeLock now uses separate chain so a rejected save
  doesn't poison future saves; error still surfaces to the failing caller
- C2: git log uses --format=%H %s%n%b%x00 with NUL separator so commit
  bodies are fetched; BREAKING CHANGE: in footers now correctly triggers
  major bumps end-to-end (added integration test to prove it)
- C3: HistoryStore.getAll validates JSON.parse result is an array;
  corrupted history files no longer crash all callers with TypeError

IMPORTANT:
- I1: github plugin guards nextVersion null before use; preRelease check
  now uses non-optional access after the guard
- I2: HistoryStore.query returns [] when from/to version not found
  instead of silently returning the full history
- I3: API key comparison uses crypto.timingSafeEqual to prevent
  timing side-channel attacks
- I4: GroupManager validates group names against /^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$/
  in all public methods to prevent path traversal
- I5: freezeAfter validated by Zod refine — invalid day names now
  throw CONFIG_ERROR instead of silently disabling the freeze
- I6: PackageJsonSource.write is now atomic via tmp + rename

MINOR:
- M2: HistoryStore.query tests added for since, from/to, last, and
  combined filters including not-found cases
- M3: inquirer removed from dependencies (never imported anywhere)
- M4: policy test description corrected (null = unlimited, not 0)
- M5: CLI --last validates positive integer; exits 1 with clear message
- M6: GroupManager.delete and path-traversal rejection now tested
- M7: api.test.ts cleans up BUMPCRAFT_API_KEY in afterEach
- M8: integration test uses vi.resetModules() in beforeEach for
  proper ESM module isolation between tests
commits.raw:
22cc5ebecbccada97dd4e450a88749656d86804d fix: address fourth-round audit — all remaining issues resolved
CRITICAL:
- C1: HistoryStore.writeLock now uses separate chain so a rejected save
  doesn't poison future saves; error still surfaces to the failing caller
- C2: git log uses --format=%H %s%n%b%x00 with NUL separator so commit
  bodies are fetched; BREAKING CHANGE: in footers now correctly triggers
  major bumps end-to-end (added integration test to prove it)
- C3: HistoryStore.getAll validates JSON.parse result is an array;
  corrupted history files no longer crash all callers with TypeError

IMPORTANT:
- I1: github plugin guards nextVersion null before use; preRelease check
  now uses non-optional access after the guard
- I2: HistoryStore.query returns [] when from/to version not found
  instead of silently returning the full history
- I3: API key comparison uses crypto.timingSafeEqual to prevent
  timing side-channel attacks
- I4: GroupManager validates group names against /^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$/
  in all public methods to prevent path traversal
- I5: freezeAfter validated by Zod refine — invalid day names now
  throw CONFIG_ERROR instead of silently disabling the freeze
- I6: PackageJsonSource.write is now atomic via tmp + rename

MINOR:
- M2: HistoryStore.query tests added for since, from/to, last, and
  combined filters including not-found cases
- M3: inquirer removed from dependencies (never imported anywhere)
- M4: policy test description corrected (null = unlimited, not 0)
- M5: CLI --last validates positive integer; exits 1 with clear message
- M6: GroupManager.delete and path-traversal rejection now tested
- M7: api.test.ts cleans up BUMPCRAFT_API_KEY in afterEach
- M8: integration test uses vi.resetModules() in beforeEach for
  proper ESM module isolation between tests

commits.subject: address third-round audit issues (N1-N9, N11-N14)
commits.body:
- N1: bumpPreRelease guards against NaN counter (non-numeric suffix like
  beta.rc no longer produces beta.NaN)
- N2: HistoryStore.query applies since filter on full list before from/to
  slicing so version lookups are accurate when filters are combined
- N3: validateReleaseBody rejects non-object bodies (string/array/null)
  to prevent validation bypass on malformed JSON payloads
- N4: GroupManager.delete wraps unlink; ENOENT is silently ignored,
  other errors surface with a descriptive message
- N5: interactive.ts defaults to vi on non-Windows; release command
  catches editor failures and continues without edits
- N6: HistoryStore atomic write via tmp file + rename to prevent
  partial-write from permanently wiping history on crash
- N7: changelog CLI exits with code 2 when no changes found, matching
  the validate command and enabling scripted pipeline detection
- N8: GET /api/history validates that last is a positive integer,
  returns 400 for non-numeric or negative values
- N9: changelogJsonPlugin falls back to 'unknown' for nextVersion so
  the version key is never silently dropped from JSON history entries
- N11: SemVer._compare uses > / < (ASCII) instead of localeCompare for
  semver spec §11.4.4 compliance
- N12: GroupManager.list uses slice(0,-5) for safe .json suffix removal
- N13: server.ts error handler gives a user-friendly message on EADDRINUSE
- N14: integration test uses git init -b main to work regardless of
  the user's init.defaultBranch git config
commits.raw:
d662b0b90ef6cf4e87631050c66e3d54b1fc1b3e fix: address third-round audit issues (N1-N9, N11-N14)
- N1: bumpPreRelease guards against NaN counter (non-numeric suffix like
  beta.rc no longer produces beta.NaN)
- N2: HistoryStore.query applies since filter on full list before from/to
  slicing so version lookups are accurate when filters are combined
- N3: validateReleaseBody rejects non-object bodies (string/array/null)
  to prevent validation bypass on malformed JSON payloads
- N4: GroupManager.delete wraps unlink; ENOENT is silently ignored,
  other errors surface with a descriptive message
- N5: interactive.ts defaults to vi on non-Windows; release command
  catches editor failures and continues without edits
- N6: HistoryStore atomic write via tmp file + rename to prevent
  partial-write from permanently wiping history on crash
- N7: changelog CLI exits with code 2 when no changes found, matching
  the validate command and enabling scripted pipeline detection
- N8: GET /api/history validates that last is a positive integer,
  returns 400 for non-numeric or negative values
- N9: changelogJsonPlugin falls back to 'unknown' for nextVersion so
  the version key is never silently dropped from JSON history entries
- N11: SemVer._compare uses > / < (ASCII) instead of localeCompare for
  semver spec §11.4.4 compliance
- N12: GroupManager.list uses slice(0,-5) for safe .json suffix removal
- N13: server.ts error handler gives a user-friendly message on EADDRINUSE
- N14: integration test uses git init -b main to work regardless of
  the user's init.defaultBranch git config

commits.subject: address second-round audit issues (C1-C3, I1-I9 + minor fixes)
commits.body:
- C1: HistoryStore.save serializes writes via a promise chain to prevent
  concurrent read-modify-write corruption
- C2: GroupManager.create re-throws non-ENOENT errors instead of silently
  overwriting group data on permission errors or corrupt files
- C3: config.ts only suppresses ENOENT; SyntaxError in JSON config now
  throws BumpcraftError(CONFIG_ERROR) instead of falling back to defaults
- I1: PipelineRunner uses continue instead of break at changelog stage so
  release/notify stages still run when a resolve plugin sets nextVersion
- I2: CLI release command locks in the policy-checked bumpType for the
  actual release call to prevent race-condition over/under-bumping
- I3: GitHub plugin throws on non-422 API failures (propagates via
  PipelineRunner error isolation) instead of silently returning
- I4: authMiddleware uses 401 for missing key (no env var name leakage),
  403 only for wrong key; generic error messages throughout
- I5: HistoryStore.query returns empty array when since version not found
  instead of returning all entries
- I6: group release preserves group when commits produce no releasable bump
- I7: SemVer._compare correctly handles numeric-vs-alphanumeric pre-release
  identifiers per semver spec §11.4.1
- I8: dashboard HTML cached at app startup (readFileSync once) instead of
  blocking the event loop on every request
- I9: dashboard uses textContent/createElement instead of innerHTML to
  prevent XSS via API response values
- Minor: openInEditor cleans up temp file in finally block
commits.raw:
9cca77ae0d982f7e1a980be82b9ab3a4e4095b5e fix: address second-round audit issues (C1-C3, I1-I9 + minor fixes)
- C1: HistoryStore.save serializes writes via a promise chain to prevent
  concurrent read-modify-write corruption
- C2: GroupManager.create re-throws non-ENOENT errors instead of silently
  overwriting group data on permission errors or corrupt files
- C3: config.ts only suppresses ENOENT; SyntaxError in JSON config now
  throws BumpcraftError(CONFIG_ERROR) instead of falling back to defaults
- I1: PipelineRunner uses continue instead of break at changelog stage so
  release/notify stages still run when a resolve plugin sets nextVersion
- I2: CLI release command locks in the policy-checked bumpType for the
  actual release call to prevent race-condition over/under-bumping
- I3: GitHub plugin throws on non-422 API failures (propagates via
  PipelineRunner error isolation) instead of silently returning
- I4: authMiddleware uses 401 for missing key (no env var name leakage),
  403 only for wrong key; generic error messages throughout
- I5: HistoryStore.query returns empty array when since version not found
  instead of returning all entries
- I6: group release preserves group when commits produce no releasable bump
- I7: SemVer._compare correctly handles numeric-vs-alphanumeric pre-release
  identifiers per semver spec §11.4.1
- I8: dashboard HTML cached at app startup (readFileSync once) instead of
  blocking the event loop on every request
- I9: dashboard uses textContent/createElement instead of innerHTML to
  prevent XSS via API response values
- Minor: openInEditor cleans up temp file in finally block

commits.subject: address important audit issues (I1/I2/I3/I5/I6/I7/I10)
commits.body:
- I1: wrap PackageJsonSource.write() in try/catch with BumpcraftError
- I2: enforce maxBumpPerDay in PolicyEngine.check(bumpType, todayCount)
  and compute today's release count in the CLI before checking
- I3: add requiresConfirmation to PolicyResult based on autoRelease list;
  CLI auto-enables interactive mode when bumpType not in autoRelease
- I5: freezeAfter now blocks 1–2 days after freeze day (covers weekend)
- I6: GitHub plugin handles 422 by fetching the existing release
- I7: getCommitsSince uses git.raw(['log', range, '--format=%H %s'])
  for reliable output instead of simple-git's structured log parser
- I10: validate preRelease/forceBump/from on POST /api/release and
  /api/release/dry-run, returning 400 on invalid input
commits.raw:
da086ab4ad9c5432d7b84550948f9a2fdd8973d9 fix: address important audit issues (I1/I2/I3/I5/I6/I7/I10)
- I1: wrap PackageJsonSource.write() in try/catch with BumpcraftError
- I2: enforce maxBumpPerDay in PolicyEngine.check(bumpType, todayCount)
  and compute today's release count in the CLI before checking
- I3: add requiresConfirmation to PolicyResult based on autoRelease list;
  CLI auto-enables interactive mode when bumpType not in autoRelease
- I5: freezeAfter now blocks 1–2 days after freeze day (covers weekend)
- I6: GitHub plugin handles 422 by fetching the existing release
- I7: getCommitsSince uses git.raw(['log', range, '--format=%H %s'])
  for reliable output instead of simple-git's structured log parser
- I10: validate preRelease/forceBump/from on POST /api/release and
  /api/release/dry-run, returning 400 on invalid input

commits.subject: add integration tests and Docker setup
commits.body: Adds full-pipeline integration test against a temp git repo, Dockerfile and docker-compose for the API, and fixes Zod v4 / simple-git TypeScript compatibility (z.record now requires 2 args, object .default() requires factory, simpleGit changed to named import).

Adds full-pipeline integration test against a temp git repo, Dockerfile and docker-compose for the API, and fixes Zod v4 / simple-git TypeScript compatibility (z.record now requires 2 args, object .default() requires factory, simpleGit changed to named import).

commits.subject: scaffold bumpcraft project structure
commits.body:
Initialize bumpcraft as an ESM TypeScript project with npm, configure
TypeScript (NodeNext modules), Vitest for testing, and set up all source
and test directory trees for the pluggable semver engine.

## 1.0.1 (2026-03-18)

### Documentation

- add deployment guide, update docker.md and README ([1d9c531])
- add comprehensive documentation ([7cbabc7])

### Bug Fixes

- address seventh-round audit — dashboard build + query param safety ([f1c43f1])
- address sixth-round audit — last two stragglers ([e8e6cf9])
- address fifth-round audit — final hardening ([8a39921])
- address fourth-round audit — all remaining issues resolved BREAKING ([22cc5eb])
- address third-round audit issues (N1-N9, N11-N14) ([d662b0b])
- address second-round audit issues (C1-C3, I1-I9 + minor fixes) ([9cca77a])
- address important audit issues (I1/I2/I3/I5/I6/I7/I10) ([da086ab])
- resolve critical audit issues (SemVer comparison, forceBump, branch strategy) ([ca2b42e])

### Features

- add integration tests and Docker setup ([b19b589])
- add REST API, CLI, library API, and release dashboard ([77fb922])
- add plugins, policy engine, history store, and release groups ([c062f9b])
- add GitClient, VersionSource, and PipelineRunner ([26f8e64])
- add core library (errors, logger, SemVer, config, BumpResolver, pipeline types) ([005634b])
- scaffold bumpcraft project structure ([d904af9])
